title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves. The bond carries a 3.5% annual coupon and matures on 18 February 2029. This issuance represents the first operation under the Bank's updated programme, which shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and greater transparency, as announced in September 2025.
key_points: Bank of England issued US$2.5 billion 3-year RegS/144A eurobond on 10 February 2026
Coupon rate is 3.5% per annum with maturity on 18 February 2029
This was the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced 23 September 2025
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
Bank shifted to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
Foreign exchange reserves are separate from Government's own foreign exchange reserves, managed by Bank as Treasury's agent
why_it_matters: This issuance marks the operational implementation of the Bank of England's revised foreign currency reserves financing framework. The shift to a structured, predictable issuance calendar with designated market makers enhances price discovery and liquidity for UK sovereign debt in foreign currency. The four-bank syndicate structure provides institutional distribution infrastructure for this benchmark offering.
market_structure_context: The Bank of England's move to a regularized two-benchmark-annual issuance timetable (announced September 2025, first executed February 2026) represents a structural change in how UK foreign currency reserves are financed. The use of a four-bank joint lead manager group (RBC, BMO, HSBC, Morgan Stanley) establishes a consistent distribution framework. RegS/144A format allows both international and US institutional participation.

issues_found: Published_at metadata (2026-03-27) reflects website indexing date, not the notice's actual issuance date (2026-02-10) - this is a minor metadata discrepancy but not a factual error in the record itself

The 3.75% Bank Rate appears in the 'important_numbers' field but is not part of this specific notice - it is sidebar/navigation content from the Bank's website unrelated to the foreign currency reserves issuance

notes: Document is a formal market notice with limited substantive content beyond issuance parameters. Majority of text is regulatory disclaimers and jurisdictional restrictions. Core archival value lies in the documented shift to structured, transparent reserve financing and the specific transaction details (size, coupon, maturity, managers). The 3.75% Bank Rate in the sidebar appears to be unrelated navigation context from the Bank's website and is not part of this specific notice.
